title: Chartered architectural technologists, planning officers and consultants
description: Plan and design structures, such as private residences, office buildings, theaters, factories, and other structural property. Apply theory and principles of civil engineering in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of structures and facilities under the direction of engineering staff or physical scientists. Perform surveying and mapping duties, usually under the direction of an engineer, surveyor, cartographer, or photogrammetrist, to obtain data used for construction, mapmaking, boundary location, mining, or other purposes. May calculate mapmaking information and create maps from source data, such as surveying notes, aerial photography, satellite data, or other maps to show topographical features, political boundaries, and other features. May verify accuracy and completeness of maps. (Based on 8 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare information regarding design, structure specifications, materials, equipment, estimated costs, or construction time.
Consult with clients to determine functional or spatial requirements of structures and meet with them to review architectural drawings.
Design or plan construction of structures such as residences, office buildings, theaters, and factories, incorporating environmentally friendly practices.
Gather information related to projects' environmental sustainability and operational efficiency, then perform predesign services like feasibility studies.
Create three-dimensional representations of designs and prepare scale drawings or architectural designs using computer-aided design (CAD) tools.
Conduct periodic on-site observations of construction work and inspect proposed building sites to determine their suitability for construction.
Develop final construction plans that detail the structure's aesthetics and construction requirements and prepare contract documents for contractors.
Monitor specialists' work and conduct inspections to ensure compliance with plans, including evaluating building structures and energy consumption systems.
Assess the feasibility of land use proposals, recommend changes, and discuss land use project purposes with planning officials.
Conduct field investigations and analyze data affecting land use, monitoring construction for adherence to regulations and documenting project activities.
Review and evaluate environmental impact reports, ensuring compliance with safety regulations and mediating community disputes involving land use.
Prepare job specification sheets, develop marketing materials, and advocate sustainability projects to improve energy efficiency and minimize pollution.
Inspect properties for hazardous materials compliance, analyze fire safety systems, and review building plans for fire safety code adherence.
Develop plans for public transportation systems to reduce carbon output and identify opportunities for sustainability improvements in building operations.
Create, prepare, or requisition reports on land use data, illustrating geographic variables like population density.
Identify and prioritize energy-saving measures, collecting and analyzing energy consumption data to provide recommendations.
Inspect new installations of energy-efficient equipment to verify compliance with specifications and monitor construction activities for environmental regulation adherence.
Develop and maintain maps and databases to ensure accuracy in geographic information systems, checking for errors and making corrections.
Educate customers about energy efficiency and answer queries on appliance costs or selections.
Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, including records of convicted arsonists and arson suspects.
Inspect and test properties and fire safety equipment to ensure compliance with applicable fire codes, ordinances, and regulations.
Participate in creating fire safety guidelines and develop fire prevention programs such as inspection reporting and hazardous materials management.
Communicate fire details and coordinate accountability among superiors and subordinate personnel using two-way radios.
Assess the extent of fire and building conditions, including the danger to adjacent structures and water supply status.
Perform administrative duties, such as maintaining records, preparing reports, and managing daily operations.
Testify in legal cases related to fires, suspected arson, and false alarms, providing expert testimony as required.
